questions.q: Which former Virginia Lieutenant Governor died in an apparent murder-suicide alongside his wife on April 16, 2026?
questions.correctText: Justin Fairfax, who served as Virginia's lieutenant governor from 2018 to 2022, fatally shot his wife and then himself in their Annandale home.

questions.q: On April 15, 2026, a Manhattan federal jury ruled that which ticketing giant and its parent company operated as an illegal monopoly over big concert venues?
questions.correctText: A jury found that Live Nation and its subsidiary Ticketmaster had a harmful monopoly over major venues, overcharging buyers by $1.72 per ticket.

questions.q: Which conservative attorney was officially disbarred by the California Supreme Court on April 15, 2026, for his role in trying to overturn the 2020 election?
questions.correctText: The California Supreme Court ordered John Eastman's name stricken from the state roll of attorneys due to his efforts to help Donald Trump block the 2020 election certification.

questions.q: Drivers filed a federal class action lawsuit against San Jose on April 15, 2026, over the city's use of nearly 500 automated license plate reader cameras operated by what tech company?
questions.correctText: The lawsuit alleges that the city's widespread deployment of Flock Safety cameras violates the Fourth Amendment by conducting warrantless tracking of vehicle movements.

questions.q: A federal jury in Miami ordered Carnival Cruise Line to pay $300,000 to an injured passenger after finding the company negligently overserved her 14 shots of what type of alcohol?
questions.correctText: Diana Sanders was awarded $300,000 after falling and suffering injuries when she was served at least 14 shots of tequila aboard the Carnival Radiance.
